title: README claims case-insensitive author filter, code uses Contains
evidence: README.md:101-102 says filter is 'case-insensitive, partial match'; Program.cs:56 uses b.Author.Contains(author) whose case sensitivity depends on SQLite's default LIKE/instr translation and is not explicitly enforced
suggestion: Use EF.Functions.Like with an explicit collation, or update the README to match actual behavior

title: Clean DTO/entity separation and shared validation
evidence: Models/BookDtos.cs BookRequest is validated (Program.cs:30,71) separately from the persisted Models/Book.cs entity, and the Validate() helper is reused by both POST and PUT
suggestion: None — this is a strength worth noting for cross-run comparison

title: Integration tests isolate DB via in-memory SQLite per factory
evidence: tests/BookCollection.Tests/ApiFactory.cs:12-30 swaps the DbContextOptions to a shared open in-memory SqliteConnection so tests never touch books.db
